Make, play, ride 🌹

Match the halves to show what your family can do.

3My family is very talented! Match 1–5 with a–e. Click left, then click right.

🌿 Beginning

0 Dad can play (f — football, example)
1 Mum can make
2 I can ride
3 My big sister can play the
4 My brother can make a
5 My baby sister can play

🌸 Ending

a guitar 🎸
b cupcakes 🧁
c a horse 🐴
d fantastic poster 🖼️
e computer games 🎮

5TV
6to music

🌸 Things I don't like

7up
8to bed
9my room

Days & Months 📅

Days of the week and months — write the missing letters.

2Complete the sentences with days of the week.
0On Tuesdays I see my friends at school. (example: Tuesdays)
1On Fmy sister swims in the sea.
2On Wwe go to Aunt Magda's house.
3On ThI finish school early.
4On Sawe work in the garden.
5On MI play football at school.

💡 Tip: type only the ending after the letters shown (e.g. ridays)

+0 ✦
3Complete the sentences with the names of the months.
0Hi, I'm Mike. My birthday is in March. (month 3)
1My friend's birthday is in(month 8)
2My mum's birthday is in(month 2)
3My dad's birthday is in(month 4)
4My cousin's birthday is in(month 10)
5My sister's birthday is in(month 6)

up early. (example)

to music in his room.

great food!

well.

very fast!

1Muma shower. (have)
2Daddinner. (make)
3Davethings. (fix)
4Dianaher homework. (do)
5Greghis room. (tidy)

6.3

Adverbs of frequency ⭐

Make sentences from the words.

3Make sentences. Write the whole sentence.
0 I / English / have / never / lessons / on Monday
→ I never have English lessons on Monday. (example)
1 always / my classmates and I / our / do / homework
2 listens / often / music / to / Nesta
3 football / sometimes / the boys / play
4 early / usually / to / grandad / my / goes / bed
5 Thursday / are / Ania / never / Ewa / late / and / on
+0 ✦
7.2

Present Simple negative 🌿

Negative sentences — don't / doesn't.

1Complete the sentences with the negative form of the verbs.
0 I want a dog but my parents don't want a dog. (example)
1 You feed your dog at seven o'clock but your neighbour his dog at seven.
2 My mum likes computer games but my grandparents computer games.
3 The lion has big teeth but the tortoise big teeth.
4 My sister and I walk to school but you to school. You ride your bike.
5 Fred and Harriet take their dog to the park but Sharon her dog to the park.
+0 ✦
7.3

Present Simple questions ❓

Questions and short answers — Do / Does.

2Add do or does to make questions.
0Do you like animals? (example)
1
these animals live in Africa?
2
Superdug have dinner with Kit?
3
your sister tidy her bedroom on Saturdays?
4
Alex and Jane watch TV in the evening?
5
his mother get up early?
+0 ✦
3Complete the dialogue with short answers.
Jim: Do you get up early?
Beth: 0 Yes, I do. I always get up early. (example)
Jim: Does your mum cook breakfast for the family?
Beth: 1 . Dad cooks breakfast.
Jim: Do your sisters ride their bikes to school?
Beth: 2 . They walk to school.
Jim: Does your friend Luke go to your school?
Beth: 3 . We go together.
Jim: Do you and Luke do your homework?
Beth: 4 . We always do it after school.
Jim: Does your swimming lesson start at five?
Beth: 5 . It starts at four.
+0 ✦
★

🌸 My daily routine by Gemma Miles

On 0 Mondays, Tuesdays, 1 , Thursdays and Fridays, I 2 up early and have a 3 . Then I have breakfast and go to school. After school, I 4 my homework. In the evening, I have 5 or listen 6 music. I go to bed early. On 7 and Sundays I hang 8 with my friends.

0 I do karate after school.
→ He does karate after school. (example)
1 I have eggs for breakfast.
She
2 I tidy my bedroom every Saturday.
He
3 I watch TV in the evening.
She
4 I fix cars and bikes.
He

5Make sentences. Add the correct adverb of frequency.
Frequency adverbs: ★★★★ always    ★★★ usually    ★★ often    ★ sometimes    ☆ never
0 I / my / bike / to / ride / school (★★) → I often ride my bike to school.
1 go / to / I / early / on / bed / Monday (★★★★)
2 TV / I / in / watch / the evening (★)
3 in / cook / breakfast / the morning / I (☆)
4 read / in / I / a book / the evening (★★★)
+0 ✦

→ It's at ten past six. (example)
1 What time is the match? 05:30
2 What time is our English lesson? 09:00
3 What time is breakfast? 07:55
4 What time is lunch? 12:45
5 What time is bedtime? 10:15

Listening 🎶

Listen carefully and choose the correct answer.

1Listen and tick the correct answer (Track 6).
🎵 track 6
0 What can Adam do? (example → a)
a🎨draw
b🎂bake
c🍳cook
1 Where is Sam's sister?
a🏠at home
b🏐playing volleyball
c🌲in the forest
2 What can Mark play?
a🎹piano
b🛹skateboard
c⚽football
3 What club is on Wednesday?
a🎨art
b🏊swimming
c🎻music
4 What time is the football match on TV?
a🕗8:00
b🕟4:30
c🕠5:30
5 What does Ellen's dad do in the evening?
a📺watches TV
b📖reads a book
c🎮plays games

💡 Listen to the audio and click the correct answer. The exact answers depend on what you hear!

+0 ✦
2Listen and write T (true) or F (false) (Track 7).
🎵 track 7

👨‍👩‍👧 Family: Mum, Dad, Granny Sophie, Grandad Alex, Louis (brother)

0Mum can't cook.→ F (example)
1Mum can fix cars and bikes.
2Dad cooks dinner at the weekend.
3Granny Sophie is my father's mother.
4Grandad Alex can play the guitar.
5Louis likes sport.

💡 These are suggested answers — pick what you hear in the audio!

3Complete the answers in the dialogues.
phrase bouquet: I agree. It's at four o'clock. It's not a good idea. It's quarter to seven. She can run fast. The guitar. Yes, let's do that!
0 A: Let's do something fun!
B: Yes, let's do that! (example)
1 A: What can your sister play?
B:
2 A: What time is the match?
B:
3 A: We can go ice-skating on Saturday.
B: . I can't ice-skate.
4 A: Let's go swimming.
B: . It's a hot day.
5 A: What time is it?
B:
